title: File open/close/read/write BIFs via completion IO
purpose: This brief provides the basic file I/O BIFs that Erlang and Gleam code uses for reading and writing files. Each operation submits to the CompletionRing (B-100), suspends the calling process via the IoFacility (B-101), and resumes with the result when the completion arrives. File descriptors are managed as FdResource terms (B-102). The BIF surface matches the erlang:open/close/read/write subset that OTP's file module delegates to — not the full file module API (seek, pread, truncate are B-104).
task: Implement file_open, file_close, file_read, and file_write as native BIFs. file_open submits an Openat operation and returns an FdResource term. file_close submits a Close operation. file_read and file_write submit Read/Write operations and suspend the process until completion. Handle error mapping from OS errno to Erlang-style {error, Reason} tuples.

requirements.title: file_open BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:open_file/2 is called with a filename binary and mode flags, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit an Openat operation to the CompletionRing, suspend the calling process, and on completion return an FdResource term or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:open_file/2 registered as BIF with args (Filename :: binary(), Modes :: list())
Modes supported: [read], [write], [append], [read, write], [{create, true}], [{truncate, true}]
Mode list parsed to libc O_* flags (O_RDONLY, O_WRONLY, O_RDWR, O_CREAT, O_TRUNC, O_APPEND)
Default file permissions: 0o644 for created files
On success: FdResource term allocated on process heap with owner_pid = calling process
On failure: {error, Reason} tuple where Reason is atom (enoent, eacces, eexist, etc.)
Errno to atom mapping for common errors
Test: open existing file for read, verify FdResource returned
Test: open nonexistent file, verify {error, enoent}

requirements.title: file_close BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:close_file/1 is called with an FdResource term, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit a Close operation to the CompletionRing and return ok or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:close_file/1 registered as BIF
Validates argument is an FdResource term
Calls FdInner::explicit_close() which submits async Close to the ring
Process suspends until close completes
Returns ok on success, {error, Reason} on failure
Closing an already-closed FdResource returns {error, closed}
Test: open file, close it, verify ok returned

requirements.title: file_read BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:read_file/2 is called with an FdResource and byte count, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit a Read operation to the CompletionRing, suspend the process, and on completion return {ok, Binary} or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:read_file/2 registered as BIF with args (Fd :: fd_resource(), Count :: non_neg_integer())
Submits Read { fd, buf_len: count, offset: current_position } to the ring
Process suspends via IoFacility
On completion: allocate binary term on process heap from read bytes, return {ok, Binary}
On EOF: return {ok, <<>>} (empty binary)
On error: return {error, Reason}
Test: write a temp file, open for read, read contents, verify match

requirements.title: file_write BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:write_file/2 is called with an FdResource and a binary, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit a Write operation to the CompletionRing, suspend the process, and on completion return ok or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:write_file/2 registered as BIF with args (Fd :: fd_resource(), Data :: binary())
Extracts bytes from binary term via Binary::as_bytes()
Submits Write { fd, data: bytes.to_vec(), offset: current_position } to the ring
Process suspends via IoFacility
On completion: return ok if all bytes written
On partial write: return {error, {incomplete, BytesWritten}}
On error: return {error, Reason}
Test: open file for write, write binary, close, reopen and read, verify content

requirements.title: Errno to Erlang atom mapping
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a function that maps OS errno values to Erlang-standard error reason atoms.
acceptance: errno_to_atom(errno: i32) -> Atom function in io/mod.rs
Mappings: ENOENT->enoent, EACCES->eacces, EEXIST->eexist, EISDIR->eisdir, ENOTDIR->enotdir, ENOSPC->enospc, EMFILE->emfile, ENFILE->enfile, EBADF->ebadf, EPIPE->epipe, EAGAIN->eagain, EINVAL->einval
Unknown errno: returns atom 'unknown_error'
Common atoms added to atom table: enoent, eacces, eexist, eisdir, enospc, ebadf, epipe, eagain
Test: verify mapping for each listed errno

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement seek, pread, pwrite — those are B-104
SHALL NOT implement stat, list_dir, mkdir — those are B-105
SHALL NOT implement the full OTP file module API — only the core primitives
SHALL NOT manage file positions internally — offset tracking is the caller's responsibility for now (B-104 adds positional IO)
verification: cargo test passes with file open/read/write/close cycle
Temp file round-trip: write data, close, reopen, read, verify data matches
Error handling: open nonexistent file returns {error, enoent}
Process exits with open FD — FD is closed by FdResource lifecycle (B-102)
Multiple processes can have concurrent file operations without interference
